Farmers grow same crops in different seasons.
A natural fibre that comes from animals.
A bird that helps farmers by eating locusts.
The motion of Earth that causes day and night.
A flower from Mexico used in Indian festivals.
A practice from India now followed globally for health.

questions.title: Assertion (A): The Earth rotates to cause day and night. Reason (R): The Sun moves from east to west in the sky.
options: Both A and R are true, and R correctly explains A.
Both A and R are true, but R does not explain A.
A is true, but R is false.
A is false, but R is true.

questions.text: Which is the largest mangrove forest in the world?
Which ancient Indian system guides the placement of rooms, doors and windows to harness natural energy?
What is the special wool called that comes from the Changthangi goat of Ladakh?
Which village in Arunachal Pradesh is known as India's 'First Village of the Sunrise'?
What does 'Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam' mean?
What is the name given to the natural bridges formed by the aerial roots of trees across streams?
Which airport in Kerala became the first in the world to be entirely powered by solar energy?
What do we call the hand-spun, hand-woven cloth symbolising self-sufficiency and India's freedom struggle?
What is the source of energy for all living things?
Which place in Meghalaya is described as the wettest inhabited place on Earth?
How did the 'Save Silent Valley' movement stop the dam project? Why is this victory important for protecting forests?
How does Cochin International Airport use solar power? What benefits does this bring for the environment and local economy?
What are the main steps in making Khadi – from cotton yarn to finished cloth. Why is Khadi so significant in Indian culture?
Why is Mawsynram called the wettest place on Earth? How does its heavy rainfall affect the daily life of its residents?
How has the DIGIPIN digital address system changed the way we find locations in India? What advantages does it offer?
What alternative practice do people in our country follow instead of discarding clothes?
What do we usually do when a piece of clothing no longer fits us?
What might an elder make from old clothes?
How are traditional quilts made, according to the passage?
Unscramble ADLINS. Hint: Land completely surrounded by water.
Unscramble LAROS. Hint: Energy from the Sun.
Unscramble MOOL. Hint: Tool used for weaving cloth.
Unscramble NMOOSNO. Hint: Seasonal wind bringing heavy rain.
Unscramble DERBORS. Hint: Divisions between countries that vanish from space.
